input.content:
# Implementation Plan: Guild Operations System

**Branch**: `001-define-specification-driven` | **Date**: 2026-04-08 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-define-specification-driven/spec.md`

## Summary

Build a specification-driven guild operations tool for WoW TBC Classic that unifies economic planning (auction house deficit analysis with BUY/FARM/WAIT recommendations) and raid readiness validation (READY/WARNING/NOT_READY player classification) against formal raid preparation contracts. Python CLI application with SQLite storage, ingesting AH snapshots and addon-exported character profiles, producing deterministic outputs for guild coordination.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.11+  
**Primary Dependencies**: Typer (CLI), Pydantic (data validation), httpx (HTTP client), PyYAML (config), SQLAlchemy or raw sqlite3 (ORM/DB)  
**Storage**: SQLite (embedded, single `.db` file)  
**Testing**: pytest + syrupy (snapshot testing)  
**Target Platform**: Cross-platform local CLI (Windows, macOS, Linux)  
**Project Type**: CLI tool  
**Performance Goals**: Deficit report generation < 30 seconds for 40-player roster; readiness evaluation < 5 seconds per player  
**Constraints**: Deterministic outputs (same input = byte-identical output); no network dependency at evaluation time (data pre-ingested)  
**Scale/Scope**: Single guild, up to 40 players, up to 15 requirements per role, up to 16 concurrent raid contracts

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

The project constitution is an unfilled template — no gates defined. No violations to evaluate.

**Post-Phase 1 re-check**: Constitution remains unfilled. No violations.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-define-specification-driven/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output
│   ├── ingestion.md     # Data ingestion contracts
│   ├── evaluation.md    # Readiness & economic evaluation contracts
│   └── reporting.md     # Output & reporting contracts
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md  # Spec quality checklist
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── guild_ops/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── cli.py              # Typer CLI entry point
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── item.py          # Item, Consumable, Material, Recipe
│   │   ├── character.py     # CharacterProfile, GuildRoster
│   │   ├── contract.py      # RaidPreparationContract, RoleRequirement
│   │   ├── market.py        # AuctionHouseSnapshot, PricePoint
│   │   ├── evaluation.py    # ReadinessClassification, EconomicRecommendation, Violation
│   │   └── drift.py         # DriftEvent, Baseline
│   ├── ingestion/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── auction_house.py # AH data ingestion (Blizzard API, NexusHub, file)
│   │   ├── character.py     # Character profile ingestion (addon export, API)
│   │   └── guild_bank.py    # Guild bank data ingestion
│   ├── engine/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── deficit.py       # Guild-level deficit computation
│   │   ├── economics.py     # BUY/FARM/WAIT recommendation engine
│   │   ├── readiness.py     # Player readiness evaluation
│   │   └── drift.py         # Drift detection engine
│   ├── reporting/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── summary.py       # Guild readiness summary report
│   │   └── formatters.py    # Output formatting (text, JSON)
│   ├── storage/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── database.py      # SQLite connection and schema management
│   │   └── seed.py          # Item database seeding from YAML
│   └── config.py            # Configuration loading and validation
│
├── data/
│   ├── items/
│   │   ├── consumables.yaml # TBC consumables catalog
│   │   ├── materials.yaml   # Raw materials catalog
│   │   └── recipes.yaml     # Crafting recipe relationships
│   └── contracts/
│       └── sample_karazhan.yaml  # Sample raid contract
│
tests/
├── conftest.py              # Shared fixtures (sample data, test DB)
├── unit/
│   ├── test_deficit.py
│   ├── test_economics.py
│   ├── test_readiness.py
│   └── test_drift.py
├── integration/
│   ├── test_ingestion.py
│   ├── test_evaluation_pipeline.py
│   └── test_determinism.py
└── fixtures/
    ├── auction_snapshot.json
    ├── character_profiles.json
    └── sample_contract.yaml
```

**Structure Decision**: Single-project CLI layout. The `engine/` package isolates pure computation (deficit, economics, readiness, drift) from I/O concerns (`ingestion/`, `storage/`, `reporting/`). Models are Pydantic dataclasses in `models/`. This separation ensures the evaluation engine is testable with no I/O dependencies, supporting the determinism requirement.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations to justify — constitution is unfilled.

# Data Model: Guild Operations System

**Branch**: `001-define-specification-driven` | **Date**: 2026-04-08

## Entity Relationship Overview

```
RaidPreparationContract ──┬── has many ──▶ RoleRequirement
                          ├── has one  ──▶ GuildRoster
                          └── has many ──▶ GuildMaterialRequirement

GuildRoster ── has many ──▶ RosterEntry ── links to ──▶ CharacterProfile

CharacterProfile ──┬── has many ──▶ EquippedItem
                   ├── has many ──▶ InventoryItem
                   └── has many ──▶ Enchantment

RoleRequirement ──┬── has many ──▶ RequiredConsumable
                  ├── has many ──▶ RequiredEnchantment
                  └── has one  ──▶ GearMinimum

AuctionHouseSnapshot ── has many ──▶ AuctionListing ── references ──▶ Item

Item ──┬── subtype ──▶ Consumable
       ├── subtype ──▶ Material
       └── has many ──▶ Recipe (as output)

Recipe ── has many ──▶ RecipeIngredient ── references ──▶ Item (as input)

EconomicRecommendation ── references ──▶ Item
ReadinessClassification ── references ──▶ CharacterProfile
                        ── has many ──▶ Violation
DriftEvent ── references ──▶ Baseline (previous evaluation state)
```

## Entities

### Item

The base catalog entry for any game item.

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Unique game item ID (from Blizzard item database) |
| name | string | Display name |
| item_type | enum | CONSUMABLE, MATERIAL, GEAR, ENCHANT_FORMULA, OTHER |
| quality | enum | POOR, COMMON, UNCOMMON, RARE, EPIC, LEGENDARY |
| is_farmable | boolean | Whether the item can be obtained by farming (gathering, mob drops) |
| sources | list of string | Known acquisition methods (e.g., "Herbalism", "Drop: Moroes", "Vendor: Shattrath") |

**Validation**: item_id must be positive. name must be non-empty. sources may be empty for unknown items.

### Consumable (extends Item)

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| buff_category | enum | FLASK, ELIXIR_BATTLE, ELIXIR_GUARDIAN, FOOD, POTION, SCROLL, WEAPON_OIL, OTHER |
| buff_duration_minutes | integer | Duration of the buff in minutes |
| stacks_with | list of enum | Which buff categories this stacks with |
| is_mandatory_default | boolean | Whether typically required (used as a contract authoring hint) |

**Validation**: buff_duration_minutes must be positive. buff_category determines stacking rules (e.g., only one FLASK, one ELIXIR_BATTLE, one ELIXIR_GUARDIAN active).

### Material (extends Item)

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| gathering_skill | string or null | Required gathering profession (Herbalism, Mining, Skinning) or null if not gathered |
| gathering_skill_level | integer or null | Minimum skill level to gather |

### Recipe

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| recipe_id | integer | Unique recipe identifier |
| output_item_id | integer | Item produced (FK → Item) |
| output_quantity | integer | Number of items produced per craft |
| profession | string | Required crafting profession (Alchemy, Enchanting, etc.) |
| skill_level_required | integer | Minimum profession skill to craft |
| ingredients | list of RecipeIngredient | Required input materials |

### RecipeIngredient

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Input material (FK → Item) |
| quantity | integer | Number required per craft |

### CharacterProfile

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| character_id | string | Unique identifier (name-realm) |
| character_name | string | Character display name |
| realm | string | Server/realm name |
| class_name | string | Character class (Warrior, Mage, Priest, etc.) |
| level | integer | Character level |
| assigned_role | enum | TANK, HEALER, MELEE_DPS, RANGED_DPS |
| guild_rank | string | Guild rank title |
| professions | list of Profession | Character professions with skill levels |
| equipped_items | list of EquippedItem | Currently equipped gear by slot |
| inventory_consumables | list of InventoryItem | Consumables in bags |
| enchantments | list of AppliedEnchantment | Enchantments on equipped gear |
| talent_build | string | Talent spec summary (e.g., "17/44/0") |
| last_updated | datetime | Timestamp of last data export |
| officer_notes | string or null | Guild officer annotations |

**Validation**: level must be 58-70 for TBC. character_id is derived as lowercase `{name}-{realm}`. assigned_role must match valid roles.

### Profession

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| name | string | Profession name (Alchemy, Herbalism, etc.) |
| skill_level | integer | Current skill level (0-375 for TBC) |

### EquippedItem

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| slot | enum | HEAD, NECK, SHOULDER, BACK, CHEST, WRIST, HANDS, WAIST, LEGS, FEET, FINGER_1, FINGER_2, TRINKET_1, TRINKET_2, MAIN_HAND, OFF_HAND, RANGED |
| item_id | integer | Game item ID |
| item_name | string | Item display name |
| item_level | integer | Item's iLevel |
| gems | list of integer | Socketed gem item IDs |

### AppliedEnchantment

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| slot | enum | Gear slot this enchantment is applied to |
| enchant_id | integer | Enchantment ID |
| enchant_name | string | Enchantment display name |

### InventoryItem

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Game item ID |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| quantity | integer | Stack count in bags |

### RaidPreparationContract

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| contract_id | string | Unique identifier (e.g., "karazhan-2026-04-15") |
| raid_name | string | Raid instance name |
| scheduled_time | datetime | Raid start date and time |
| status | enum | DRAFT, ACTIVE, COMPLETED, CANCELLED |
| role_requirements | dict of enum → RoleRequirement | Per-role requirements keyed by role |
| guild_materials | list of GuildMaterialRequirement | Guild-level material needs |
| roster | GuildRoster | Confirmed player list |
| go_threshold_percent | integer | Minimum % READY for go recommendation (default: 80) |
| drift_detection_enabled | boolean | Whether drift monitoring is active for this contract |
| created_at | datetime | Contract creation timestamp |
| updated_at | datetime | Last modification timestamp |

**Validation**: go_threshold_percent must be 0-100. scheduled_time must be in the future when status is DRAFT or ACTIVE. role_requirements must cover at least TANK and HEALER.

### RoleRequirement

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| role | enum | TANK, HEALER, MELEE_DPS, RANGED_DPS |
| required_consumables | list of RequiredConsumable | Mandatory consumables |
| optional_consumables | list of RequiredConsumable | Advisory consumables (WARNING if missing, not NOT_READY) |
| required_enchantments | list of RequiredEnchantment | Mandatory enchantments by slot |
| min_average_item_level | integer or null | Minimum average iLevel across gear slots |
| min_item_level_per_slot | dict of enum → integer | Optional per-slot iLevel minimums |
| talent_guidelines | string or null | Descriptive talent spec guidance |

### RequiredConsumable

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Required consumable item ID |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| quantity | integer | Minimum quantity required in inventory (default: 1) |
| buff_category | enum | Which buff category this fills |

### RequiredEnchantment

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| slot | enum | Gear slot that must be enchanted |
| accepted_enchant_ids | list of integer | Any of these enchantment IDs satisfy the requirement |
| description | string | Human-readable description (e.g., "Enchant Weapon: Mongoose or Executioner") |

### GuildMaterialRequirement

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Required material item ID |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| total_quantity | integer | Total quantity needed for the raid |
| purpose | string | What it's for (e.g., "Field Repair Bot fuel", "Arcane Brilliance reagent") |

### GuildRoster

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| entries | list of RosterEntry | Confirmed players |

### RosterEntry

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| character_id | string | FK → CharacterProfile |
| assigned_role | enum | Role for this specific raid (may differ from profile default) |
| confirmed | boolean | Whether the player has confirmed attendance |

### AuctionHouseSnapshot

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| snapshot_id | string | Unique identifier |
| realm | string | Realm name |
| captured_at | datetime | Timestamp of snapshot |
| source | enum | BLIZZARD_API, NEXUSHUB, ADDON_EXPORT, MANUAL |
| listings | list of AuctionListing | All active listings |

**Validation**: captured_at must not be in the future. listings may be empty (valid but flagged).

### AuctionListing

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| auction_id | integer | Unique auction ID |
| item_id | integer | FK → Item |
| quantity | integer | Number of items in this listing |
| buyout_copper | integer | Buyout price in copper (0 if no buyout) |
| unit_price_copper | integer | Computed: buyout_copper / quantity |
| time_remaining | enum | SHORT, MEDIUM, LONG, VERY_LONG |

### PriceTrend

Derived from multiple AuctionHouseSnapshot records for a single item.

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | FK → Item |
| current_price_copper | integer | Latest median unit price |
| moving_average_copper | integer | Simple moving average over lookback window |
| trend_direction | enum | RISING, STABLE, FALLING |
| percent_vs_average | float | Current price as % of moving average (e.g., 0.70 = 30% below) |
| lookback_days | integer | Window used for moving average |
| sample_count | integer | Number of snapshots in the window |

### EconomicRecommendation

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | FK → Item |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| action | enum | BUY, FARM, WAIT |
| reason | string | Human-readable explanation |
| current_price_copper | integer | Current market price |
| average_price_copper | integer | Moving average price |
| deficit_quantity | integer | How many are needed |
| estimated_cost_copper | integer | deficit_quantity × current_price_copper |
| confidence | enum | HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW | Based on data quality (sample count, freshness) |

### ReadinessClassification

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| character_id | string | FK → CharacterProfile |
| contract_id | string | FK → RaidPreparationContract |
| status | enum | READY, WARNING, NOT_READY |
| violations | list of Violation | All identified issues |
| evaluated_at | datetime | Evaluation timestamp |

### Violation

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| category | enum | CONSUMABLE, ENCHANTMENT, GEAR, TALENT, PROFILE |
| severity | enum | MANDATORY (→ NOT_READY), ADVISORY (→ WARNING) |
| description | string | What is missing or wrong |
| remediation | string | How to fix it |
| market_info | string or null | Current AH availability and cost if applicable |

### DriftEvent

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| drift_id | string | Unique identifier |
| contract_id | string | FK → RaidPreparationContract |
| detected_at | datetime | When drift was detected |
| drift_type | enum | ECONOMIC, PLAYER_READINESS |
| subject | string | What drifted (item name or character ID) |
| previous_state | string | State at baseline |
| current_state | string | State now |
| impact | string | Effect on guild readiness |
| recommended_action | string | Corrective action |

### Baseline

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| baseline_id | string | Unique identifier |
| contract_id | string | FK → RaidPreparationContract |
| captured_at | datetime | When baseline was captured |
| player_classifications | list of ReadinessClassification | Player states at baseline |
| economic_recommendations | list of EconomicRecommendation | Economic states at baseline |

## State Transitions

### RaidPreparationContract.status

```
DRAFT → ACTIVE → COMPLETED
  │                  ↑
  │       ACTIVE → CANCELLED
  └──────────────→ CANCELLED
```

- DRAFT: Contract being authored, not yet enforced
- ACTIVE: Contract is the live specification; readiness checks and drift detection run against it
- COMPLETED: Raid has occurred; contract is archived
- CANCELLED: Raid cancelled; contract archived without execution

### ReadinessClassification.status

Computed on each evaluation — not a persisted state machine. Classification is purely a function of current profile state vs. current contract requirements:

- **READY**: Zero mandatory violations
- **WARNING**: Zero mandatory violations, one or more advisory violations
- **NOT_READY**: One or more mandatory violations

### EconomicRecommendation.action

Computed from price trend thresholds (configurable):

- **BUY**: `percent_vs_average <= buy_threshold` (default: 0.85, i.e., price is ≥15% below average)
- **WAIT**: `percent_vs_average >= wait_threshold` (default: 1.30, i.e., price is ≥30% above average) AND `trend_direction == RISING`
- **FARM**: All other cases where the item `is_farmable == true`, OR price is above wait_threshold and item is not farmable but trend is STABLE/FALLING
- **WAIT** (fallback): Price is above wait_threshold, item is not farmable, and trend is RISING

# Implementation Plan: Guild Operations System

**Branch**: `001-define-specification-driven` | **Date**: 2026-04-08 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-define-specification-driven/spec.md`

## Summary

Build a specification-driven guild operations tool for WoW TBC Classic that unifies economic planning (auction house deficit analysis with BUY/FARM/WAIT recommendations) and raid readiness validation (READY/WARNING/NOT_READY player classification) against formal raid preparation contracts. Python CLI application with SQLite storage, ingesting AH snapshots and addon-exported character profiles, producing deterministic outputs for guild coordination.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.11+  
**Primary Dependencies**: Typer (CLI), Pydantic (data validation), httpx (HTTP client), PyYAML (config), SQLAlchemy or raw sqlite3 (ORM/DB)  
**Storage**: SQLite (embedded, single `.db` file)  
**Testing**: pytest + syrupy (snapshot testing)  
**Target Platform**: Cross-platform local CLI (Windows, macOS, Linux)  
**Project Type**: CLI tool  
**Performance Goals**: Deficit report generation < 30 seconds for 40-player roster; readiness evaluation < 5 seconds per player  
**Constraints**: Deterministic outputs (same input = byte-identical output); no network dependency at evaluation time (data pre-ingested)  
**Scale/Scope**: Single guild, up to 40 players, up to 15 requirements per role, up to 16 concurrent raid contracts

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

The project constitution is an unfilled template — no gates defined. No violations to evaluate.

**Post-Phase 1 re-check**: Constitution remains unfilled. No violations.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-define-specification-driven/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output
│   ├── ingestion.md     # Data ingestion contracts
│   ├── evaluation.md    # Readiness & economic evaluation contracts
│   └── reporting.md     # Output & reporting contracts
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md  # Spec quality checklist
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── guild_ops/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── cli.py              # Typer CLI entry point
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── item.py          # Item, Consumable, Material, Recipe
│   │   ├── character.py     # CharacterProfile, GuildRoster
│   │   ├── contract.py      # RaidPreparationContract, RoleRequirement
│   │   ├── market.py        # AuctionHouseSnapshot, PricePoint
│   │   ├── evaluation.py    # ReadinessClassification, EconomicRecommendation, Violation
│   │   └── drift.py         # DriftEvent, Baseline
│   ├── ingestion/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── auction_house.py # AH data ingestion (Blizzard API, NexusHub, file)
│   │   ├── character.py     # Character profile ingestion (addon export, API)
│   │   └── guild_bank.py    # Guild bank data ingestion
│   ├── engine/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── deficit.py       # Guild-level deficit computation
│   │   ├── economics.py     # BUY/FARM/WAIT recommendation engine
│   │   ├── readiness.py     # Player readiness evaluation
│   │   └── drift.py         # Drift detection engine
│   ├── reporting/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── summary.py       # Guild readiness summary report
│   │   └── formatters.py    # Output formatting (text, JSON)
│   ├── storage/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── database.py      # SQLite connection and schema management
│   │   └── seed.py          # Item database seeding from YAML
│   └── config.py            # Configuration loading and validation
│
├── data/
│   ├── items/
│   │   ├── consumables.yaml # TBC consumables catalog
│   │   ├── materials.yaml   # Raw materials catalog
│   │   └── recipes.yaml     # Crafting recipe relationships
│   └── contracts/
│       └── sample_karazhan.yaml  # Sample raid contract
│
tests/
├── conftest.py              # Shared fixtures (sample data, test DB)
├── unit/
│   ├── test_deficit.py
│   ├── test_economics.py
│   ├── test_readiness.py
│   └── test_drift.py
├── integration/
│   ├── test_ingestion.py
│   ├── test_evaluation_pipeline.py
│   └── test_determinism.py
└── fixtures/
    ├── auction_snapshot.json
    ├── character_profiles.json
    └── sample_contract.yaml
```

**Structure Decision**: Single-project CLI layout. The `engine/` package isolates pure computation (deficit, economics, readiness, drift) from I/O concerns (`ingestion/`, `storage/`, `reporting/`). Models are Pydantic dataclasses in `models/`. This separation ensures the evaluation engine is testable with no I/O dependencies, supporting the determinism requirement.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations to justify — constitution is unfilled.

# Data Model: Guild Operations System

**Branch**: `001-define-specification-driven` | **Date**: 2026-04-08

## Entity Relationship Overview

```
RaidPreparationContract ──┬── has many ──▶ RoleRequirement
                          ├── has one  ──▶ GuildRoster
                          └── has many ──▶ GuildMaterialRequirement

GuildRoster ── has many ──▶ RosterEntry ── links to ──▶ CharacterProfile

CharacterProfile ──┬── has many ──▶ EquippedItem
                   ├── has many ──▶ InventoryItem
                   └── has many ──▶ Enchantment

RoleRequirement ──┬── has many ──▶ RequiredConsumable
                  ├── has many ──▶ RequiredEnchantment
                  └── has one  ──▶ GearMinimum

AuctionHouseSnapshot ── has many ──▶ AuctionListing ── references ──▶ Item

Item ──┬── subtype ──▶ Consumable
       ├── subtype ──▶ Material
       └── has many ──▶ Recipe (as output)

Recipe ── has many ──▶ RecipeIngredient ── references ──▶ Item (as input)

EconomicRecommendation ── references ──▶ Item
ReadinessClassification ── references ──▶ CharacterProfile
                        ── has many ──▶ Violation
DriftEvent ── references ──▶ Baseline (previous evaluation state)
```

## Entities

### Item

The base catalog entry for any game item.

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Unique game item ID (from Blizzard item database) |
| name | string | Display name |
| item_type | enum | CONSUMABLE, MATERIAL, GEAR, ENCHANT_FORMULA, OTHER |
| quality | enum | POOR, COMMON, UNCOMMON, RARE, EPIC, LEGENDARY |
| is_farmable | boolean | Whether the item can be obtained by farming (gathering, mob drops) |
| sources | list of string | Known acquisition methods (e.g., "Herbalism", "Drop: Moroes", "Vendor: Shattrath") |

**Validation**: item_id must be positive. name must be non-empty. sources may be empty for unknown items.

### Consumable (extends Item)

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| buff_category | enum | FLASK, ELIXIR_BATTLE, ELIXIR_GUARDIAN, FOOD, POTION, SCROLL, WEAPON_OIL, OTHER |
| buff_duration_minutes | integer | Duration of the buff in minutes |
| stacks_with | list of enum | Which buff categories this stacks with |
| is_mandatory_default | boolean | Whether typically required (used as a contract authoring hint) |

**Validation**: buff_duration_minutes must be positive. buff_category determines stacking rules (e.g., only one FLASK, one ELIXIR_BATTLE, one ELIXIR_GUARDIAN active).

### Material (extends Item)

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| gathering_skill | string or null | Required gathering profession (Herbalism, Mining, Skinning) or null if not gathered |
| gathering_skill_level | integer or null | Minimum skill level to gather |

### Recipe

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| recipe_id | integer | Unique recipe identifier |
| output_item_id | integer | Item produced (FK → Item) |
| output_quantity | integer | Number of items produced per craft |
| profession | string | Required crafting profession (Alchemy, Enchanting, etc.) |
| skill_level_required | integer | Minimum profession skill to craft |
| ingredients | list of RecipeIngredient | Required input materials |

### RecipeIngredient

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Input material (FK → Item) |
| quantity | integer | Number required per craft |

### CharacterProfile

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| character_id | string | Unique identifier (name-realm) |
| character_name | string | Character display name |
| realm | string | Server/realm name |
| class_name | string | Character class (Warrior, Mage, Priest, etc.) |
| level | integer | Character level |
| assigned_role | enum | TANK, HEALER, MELEE_DPS, RANGED_DPS |
| guild_rank | string | Guild rank title |
| professions | list of Profession | Character professions with skill levels |
| equipped_items | list of EquippedItem | Currently equipped gear by slot |
| inventory_consumables | list of InventoryItem | Consumables in bags |
| enchantments | list of AppliedEnchantment | Enchantments on equipped gear |
| talent_build | string | Talent spec summary (e.g., "17/44/0") |
| last_updated | datetime | Timestamp of last data export |
| officer_notes | string or null | Guild officer annotations |

**Validation**: level must be 58-70 for TBC. character_id is derived as lowercase `{name}-{realm}`. assigned_role must match valid roles.

### Profession

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| name | string | Profession name (Alchemy, Herbalism, etc.) |
| skill_level | integer | Current skill level (0-375 for TBC) |

### EquippedItem

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| slot | enum | HEAD, NECK, SHOULDER, BACK, CHEST, WRIST, HANDS, WAIST, LEGS, FEET, FINGER_1, FINGER_2, TRINKET_1, TRINKET_2, MAIN_HAND, OFF_HAND, RANGED |
| item_id | integer | Game item ID |
| item_name | string | Item display name |
| item_level | integer | Item's iLevel |
| gems | list of integer | Socketed gem item IDs |

### AppliedEnchantment

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| slot | enum | Gear slot this enchantment is applied to |
| enchant_id | integer | Enchantment ID |
| enchant_name | string | Enchantment display name |

### InventoryItem

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Game item ID |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| quantity | integer | Stack count in bags |

### RaidPreparationContract

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| contract_id | string | Unique identifier (e.g., "karazhan-2026-04-15") |
| raid_name | string | Raid instance name |
| scheduled_time | datetime | Raid start date and time |
| status | enum | DRAFT, ACTIVE, COMPLETED, CANCELLED |
| role_requirements | dict of enum → RoleRequirement | Per-role requirements keyed by role |
| guild_materials | list of GuildMaterialRequirement | Guild-level material needs |
| roster | GuildRoster | Confirmed player list |
| go_threshold_percent | integer | Minimum % READY for go recommendation (default: 80) |
| drift_detection_enabled | boolean | Whether drift monitoring is active for this contract |
| created_at | datetime | Contract creation timestamp |
| updated_at | datetime | Last modification timestamp |

**Validation**: go_threshold_percent must be 0-100. scheduled_time must be in the future when status is DRAFT or ACTIVE. role_requirements must cover at least TANK and HEALER.

### RoleRequirement

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| role | enum | TANK, HEALER, MELEE_DPS, RANGED_DPS |
| required_consumables | list of RequiredConsumable | Mandatory consumables |
| optional_consumables | list of RequiredConsumable | Advisory consumables (WARNING if missing, not NOT_READY) |
| required_enchantments | list of RequiredEnchantment | Mandatory enchantments by slot |
| min_average_item_level | integer or null | Minimum average iLevel across gear slots |
| min_item_level_per_slot | dict of enum → integer | Optional per-slot iLevel minimums |
| talent_guidelines | string or null | Descriptive talent spec guidance |

### RequiredConsumable

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Required consumable item ID |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| quantity | integer | Minimum quantity required in inventory (default: 1) |
| buff_category | enum | Which buff category this fills |

### RequiredEnchantment

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| slot | enum | Gear slot that must be enchanted |
| accepted_enchant_ids | list of integer | Any of these enchantment IDs satisfy the requirement |
| description | string | Human-readable description (e.g., "Enchant Weapon: Mongoose or Executioner") |

### GuildMaterialRequirement

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | Required material item ID |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| total_quantity | integer | Total quantity needed for the raid |
| purpose | string | What it's for (e.g., "Field Repair Bot fuel", "Arcane Brilliance reagent") |

### GuildRoster

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| entries | list of RosterEntry | Confirmed players |

### RosterEntry

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| character_id | string | FK → CharacterProfile |
| assigned_role | enum | Role for this specific raid (may differ from profile default) |
| confirmed | boolean | Whether the player has confirmed attendance |

### AuctionHouseSnapshot

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| snapshot_id | string | Unique identifier |
| realm | string | Realm name |
| captured_at | datetime | Timestamp of snapshot |
| source | enum | BLIZZARD_API, NEXUSHUB, ADDON_EXPORT, MANUAL |
| listings | list of AuctionListing | All active listings |

**Validation**: captured_at must not be in the future. listings may be empty (valid but flagged).

### AuctionListing

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| auction_id | integer | Unique auction ID |
| item_id | integer | FK → Item |
| quantity | integer | Number of items in this listing |
| buyout_copper | integer | Buyout price in copper (0 if no buyout) |
| unit_price_copper | integer | Computed: buyout_copper / quantity |
| time_remaining | enum | SHORT, MEDIUM, LONG, VERY_LONG |

### PriceTrend

Derived from multiple AuctionHouseSnapshot records for a single item.

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | FK → Item |
| current_price_copper | integer | Latest median unit price |
| moving_average_copper | integer | Simple moving average over lookback window |
| trend_direction | enum | RISING, STABLE, FALLING |
| percent_vs_average | float | Current price as % of moving average (e.g., 0.70 = 30% below) |
| lookback_days | integer | Window used for moving average |
| sample_count | integer | Number of snapshots in the window |

### EconomicRecommendation

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| item_id | integer | FK → Item |
| item_name | string | Display name |
| action | enum | BUY, FARM, WAIT |
| reason | string | Human-readable explanation |
| current_price_copper | integer | Current market price |
| average_price_copper | integer | Moving average price |
| deficit_quantity | integer | How many are needed |
| estimated_cost_copper | integer | deficit_quantity × current_price_copper |
| confidence | enum | HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW | Based on data quality (sample count, freshness) |

### ReadinessClassification

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| character_id | string | FK → CharacterProfile |
| contract_id | string | FK → RaidPreparationContract |
| status | enum | READY, WARNING, NOT_READY |
| violations | list of Violation | All identified issues |
| evaluated_at | datetime | Evaluation timestamp |

### Violation

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| category | enum | CONSUMABLE, ENCHANTMENT, GEAR, TALENT, PROFILE |
| severity | enum | MANDATORY (→ NOT_READY), ADVISORY (→ WARNING) |
| description | string | What is missing or wrong |
| remediation | string | How to fix it |
| market_info | string or null | Current AH availability and cost if applicable |

### DriftEvent

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| drift_id | string | Unique identifier |
| contract_id | string | FK → RaidPreparationContract |
| detected_at | datetime | When drift was detected |
| drift_type | enum | ECONOMIC, PLAYER_READINESS |
| subject | string | What drifted (item name or character ID) |
| previous_state | string | State at baseline |
| current_state | string | State now |
| impact | string | Effect on guild readiness |
| recommended_action | string | Corrective action |

### Baseline

| Field | Type | Description |
| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
| baseline_id | string | Unique identifier |
| contract_id | string | FK → RaidPreparationContract |
| captured_at | datetime | When baseline was captured |
| player_classifications | list of ReadinessClassification | Player states at baseline |
| economic_recommendations | list of EconomicRecommendation | Economic states at baseline |

## State Transitions

### RaidPreparationContract.status

```
DRAFT → ACTIVE → COMPLETED
  │                  ↑
  │       ACTIVE → CANCELLED
  └──────────────→ CANCELLED
```

- DRAFT: Contract being authored, not yet enforced
- ACTIVE: Contract is the live specification; readiness checks and drift detection run against it
- COMPLETED: Raid has occurred; contract is archived
- CANCELLED: Raid cancelled; contract archived without execution

### ReadinessClassification.status

Computed on each evaluation — not a persisted state machine. Classification is purely a function of current profile state vs. current contract requirements:

- **READY**: Zero mandatory violations
- **WARNING**: Zero mandatory violations, one or more advisory violations
- **NOT_READY**: One or more mandatory violations

### EconomicRecommendation.action

Computed from price trend thresholds (configurable):

- **BUY**: `percent_vs_average <= buy_threshold` (default: 0.85, i.e., price is ≥15% below average)
- **WAIT**: `percent_vs_average >= wait_threshold` (default: 1.30, i.e., price is ≥30% above average) AND `trend_direction == RISING`
- **FARM**: All other cases where the item `is_farmable == true`, OR price is above wait_threshold and item is not farmable but trend is STABLE/FALLING
- **WAIT** (fallback): Price is above wait_threshold, item is not farmable, and trend is RISING

input.content:
# Ingestion Contracts

**System**: Guild Operations System | **Date**: 2026-04-08

These contracts define the interfaces through which external data enters the system. All ingestion adapters MUST conform to these contracts regardless of data source.

---

## IC-01: Auction House Data Ingestion

**Purpose**: Ingest a point-in-time snapshot of auction house listings for a specific realm.

**Input**: Raw data from one of the supported sources (Blizzard API JSON, NexusHub API JSON, addon export file).

**Output**: A validated `AuctionHouseSnapshot` entity persisted to storage.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops ingest auctions` or scheduled trigger |
| Input format | JSON (API response) or structured text (addon export) |
| Required fields per listing | item_id (integer), quantity (integer ≥ 1), buyout_copper (integer ≥ 0) |
| Derived fields | unit_price_copper = buyout_copper / quantity (integer division, rounded down) |
| Deduplication | Listings with duplicate auction_id within a single snapshot are merged (last wins) |
| Staleness check | If snapshot timestamp is older than configured `max_snapshot_age_hours` (default: 24), emit WARNING and tag snapshot as STALE |
| Zero-listing items | If a required item (per any active contract) has zero listings, record it with a synthetic "UNAVAILABLE" marker |
| Idempotency | Re-ingesting the same snapshot (by snapshot_id or timestamp) replaces the previous version |
| Error handling | Malformed listings are skipped with a logged warning; ingestion continues. If >50% of listings are malformed, the entire snapshot is rejected |

**Postconditions**:
- Snapshot is queryable by realm and timestamp
- Price trend computation can be triggered against the new snapshot
- Items referenced in listings exist in the item catalog (unknown item_ids logged as warnings but not rejected)

---

## IC-02: Character Profile Ingestion

**Purpose**: Ingest a character's current state (gear, inventory, enchantments, talents, professions).

**Input**: Addon export file (JSON or parsed Lua SavedVariables) or Blizzard API response.

**Output**: A validated `CharacterProfile` entity persisted to storage.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops ingest profiles <file>` |
| Input format | JSON file containing one or more character profiles |
| Required fields | character_name, realm, class_name, level, equipped_items |
| Optional fields | inventory_consumables, enchantments, talent_build, professions, officer_notes |
| Identity | character_id derived as lowercase `{name}-{realm}` |
| Deduplication | If a profile for the same character_id exists, it is replaced entirely (last-write-wins) |
| Role assignment | assigned_role may be provided in the import or set/overridden via roster management |
| Staleness | last_updated field is mandatory; system flags profiles older than configured `max_profile_age_hours` (default: 48) |
| Partial data | Profiles with missing optional fields are accepted; missing inventory_consumables means consumable readiness cannot be fully evaluated (flagged in violations) |

**Postconditions**:
- Profile is queryable by character_id
- Profile is linkable to roster entries
- If inventory_consumables is missing, readiness evaluation will produce a WARNING-level violation noting incomplete data

---

## IC-03: Guild Bank Ingestion

**Purpose**: Ingest the current contents of the guild bank.

**Input**: Addon export or manual entry (JSON/YAML file).

**Output**: Guild bank inventory stored as a set of `InventoryItem` records tagged with source "GUILD_BANK".

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops ingest guild-bank <file>` |
| Input format | JSON or YAML file listing items and quantities |
| Required fields per entry | item_id (integer), quantity (integer ≥ 1) |
| Replacement | Each ingestion replaces the entire guild bank inventory (not incremental) |
| Validation | item_ids are checked against the item catalog; unknown items are logged as warnings |

**Postconditions**:
- Guild bank contents are available to deficit computation
- Deficit engine subtracts guild bank supply from total requirements

---

## IC-04: Raid Contract Ingestion

**Purpose**: Create or update a raid preparation contract from a YAML definition file.

**Input**: YAML file conforming to the contract schema.

**Output**: A validated `RaidPreparationContract` entity persisted to storage.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops contract load <file>` |
| Input format | YAML file |
| Required sections | raid_name, scheduled_time, role_requirements (at least TANK and HEALER), roster |
| Item validation | All item_ids and enchant_ids referenced in requirements MUST exist in the item catalog; unknown references cause rejection with specific error listing each unknown |
| Role validation | All roles referenced in roster entries MUST have a corresponding role_requirements section |
| Duplicate check | A contract with the same contract_id replaces the previous version |
| Status default | New contracts are created with status DRAFT |

**Postconditions**:
- Contract is queryable by contract_id
- Activating the contract (status → ACTIVE) enables readiness evaluation and deficit computation against it
- All referenced items and enchantments are resolvable in the catalog

# Evaluation Contracts

**System**: Guild Operations System | **Date**: 2026-04-08

These contracts define the interfaces for the core computation engines: economic recommendation, readiness classification, and drift detection.

---

## EC-01: Economic Recommendation Engine

**Purpose**: For each item deficit identified against an active raid contract, produce a deterministic economic recommendation (BUY, FARM, WAIT).

**Input**: 
- Active `RaidPreparationContract` with computed deficits
- Latest `AuctionHouseSnapshot` and `PriceTrend` data
- Configured economic thresholds

**Output**: List of `EconomicRecommendation` records, one per deficit item.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered by `guild-ops evaluate economics --contract <id>` |
| Determinism | Same inputs (contract, snapshot, thresholds) MUST produce identical outputs. No randomness, no system-clock dependency in logic (timestamp only in metadata) |
| Threshold defaults | buy_threshold: 0.85 (price ≤ 85% of average → BUY), wait_threshold: 1.30 (price ≥ 130% of average → WAIT) |
| Decision logic | 1. If no market data: action = FARM, confidence = LOW. 2. If percent_vs_average ≤ buy_threshold: action = BUY. 3. If percent_vs_average ≥ wait_threshold AND trend = RISING: action = WAIT. 4. If is_farmable: action = FARM. 5. Else: action = WAIT |
| Confidence | HIGH: ≥ 7 snapshots in lookback window. MEDIUM: 3-6 snapshots. LOW: < 3 snapshots or no data |
| Cost estimation | estimated_cost_copper = deficit_quantity × current_price_copper. If no market data, estimated_cost = 0 with note "no market data" |
| Ordering | Results ordered by estimated_cost_copper descending (most expensive deficits first) |
| Missing data | Items with no price data get action = FARM, confidence = LOW, reason = "No market data available" |

**Postconditions**:
- Every deficit item has exactly one recommendation
- Recommendations are usable by readiness remediation guidance (IC-02 → EC-01 linkage)

---

## EC-02: Readiness Classification Engine

**Purpose**: Evaluate each rostered player's character profile against the raid contract and classify as READY, WARNING, or NOT_READY.

**Input**:
- Active `RaidPreparationContract`
- `CharacterProfile` for each rostered player
- Current `EconomicRecommendation` data (for remediation guidance)

**Output**: List of `ReadinessClassification` records, one per rostered player.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered by `guild-ops evaluate readiness --contract <id>` |
| Determinism | Same inputs MUST produce identical outputs |
| Evaluation scope | Each player is evaluated against the role_requirements for their assigned_role in the contract |
| Classification rules | NOT_READY: ≥ 1 mandatory violation. WARNING: 0 mandatory violations, ≥ 1 advisory violation. READY: 0 violations |
| Consumable check | For each required_consumable: verify player's inventory_consumables contains item_id with quantity ≥ required. Missing → MANDATORY violation |
| Optional consumable check | For each optional_consumable: verify inventory. Missing → ADVISORY violation |
| Enchantment check | For each required_enchantment: verify player's enchantments for the specified slot contains one of accepted_enchant_ids. Missing → MANDATORY violation |
| Gear check | If min_average_item_level set: compute average iLevel across equipped slots. Below minimum → MANDATORY violation. Per-slot minimums checked individually |
| No profile data | If a rostered player has no CharacterProfile: status = NOT_READY, single violation: category=PROFILE, severity=MANDATORY, description="No profile data available" |
| Stale profile | If last_updated exceeds max_profile_age_hours: add ADVISORY violation noting data staleness |
| Missing inventory | If inventory_consumables is null/missing on profile: add ADVISORY violation "Consumable inventory data not available — cannot verify consumable readiness" |
| Remediation | Each violation includes a remediation string. For consumable/enchantment violations, remediation includes market info from EconomicRecommendation if available |
| Ordering | Results ordered by: NOT_READY first, then WARNING, then READY. Within each group, alphabetical by character_name |

**Postconditions**:
- Every rostered player has exactly one classification
- Zero false READY classifications (a player with any mandatory violation is never READY)
- Classifications are consumable by the summary report and drift detection engines

---

## EC-03: Deficit Computation Engine

**Purpose**: Compute guild-level material deficits by comparing total requirements against available supply.

**Input**:
- Active `RaidPreparationContract` (requirements + roster)
- All `CharacterProfile` records for rostered players (inventory)
- Guild bank inventory

**Output**: List of deficit records (item_id, total_required, total_available, deficit_quantity).

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered internally by economic evaluation, or directly via `guild-ops evaluate deficits --contract <id>` |
| Total required | Sum of required quantities across all rostered players for their assigned role, plus guild_materials |
| Total available | Sum of: (a) each player's inventory_consumables matching the item, (b) guild bank quantities |
| Deficit | max(0, total_required - total_available) |
| No double-counting | A player's inventory items count toward their own requirements AND the guild total, but are not counted twice |
| Zero deficits | Items where deficit = 0 are included in output (shows the guild has enough) |
| Determinism | Same inputs produce identical deficit counts |

**Postconditions**:
- Deficit list feeds into EC-01 (economic recommendations)
- Every item referenced in contract requirements appears in the deficit output

---

## EC-04: Drift Detection Engine

**Purpose**: Compare current evaluation state against a stored baseline and report changes.

**Input**:
- A stored `Baseline` (previous evaluation snapshot)
- Current `ReadinessClassification` and `EconomicRecommendation` outputs

**Output**: List of `DriftEvent` records.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered by `guild-ops evaluate drift --contract <id>` (only if drift_detection_enabled on contract) |
| Baseline creation | A baseline is automatically captured when a contract transitions to ACTIVE, and can be manually refreshed via `guild-ops baseline capture --contract <id>` |
| Player drift | For each player: if classification status changed (e.g., READY → NOT_READY), emit a PLAYER_READINESS drift event listing old status, new status, and the new/changed violations |
| Economic drift | For each deficit item: if recommendation action changed (e.g., BUY → WAIT), emit an ECONOMIC drift event listing old action, new action, price change, and impact on estimated cost |
| No drift | If no changes detected, return empty list (not an error) |
| Disabled | If drift_detection_enabled is false on the contract, the engine returns immediately with no output |
| Determinism | Same baseline + same current state = same drift events |

**Postconditions**:
- Drift events are reportable in the summary
- Drift events do not modify the baseline (baseline is immutable until explicitly refreshed)

# Reporting Contracts

**System**: Guild Operations System | **Date**: 2026-04-08

These contracts define the output interfaces — how computed results are presented to users.

---

## RC-01: Guild Readiness Summary Report

**Purpose**: Produce a consolidated report showing overall guild readiness for a specific raid contract.

**Input**:
- `ReadinessClassification` results for all rostered players
- `EconomicRecommendation` results for all deficit items
- `DriftEvent` list (if drift detection enabled)
- Contract's go_threshold_percent

**Output**: Structured report (text or JSON format).

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | `guild-ops report summary --contract <id>` with optional `--format text|json` |
| Sections | 1. Header (raid name, time, roster size). 2. Go/No-Go verdict. 3. Player status breakdown. 4. Deficit & economic summary. 5. Drift alerts (if any). 6. Action items |
| Go/No-Go logic | GO if (count of READY / total rostered) × 100 ≥ go_threshold_percent. Otherwise NO-GO |
| Player breakdown | Count and percentage for each status (READY, WARNING, NOT_READY). List each non-READY player with their violations |
| Economic summary | Total estimated gold cost to close all deficits. Each deficit item with quantity, unit price, total cost, and recommendation |
| Drift section | Only included if drift_detection_enabled and drift events exist |
| Action items | Prioritized list of remediation actions, ordered by impact (NOT_READY players first, then highest-cost deficits) |
| Determinism | Same inputs produce byte-identical text output (stable ordering, no timestamps in body except evaluation time in header) |
| Gold formatting | All copper values rendered as gold/silver/copper (e.g., 385012 copper → "38g 50s 12c") |

**Postconditions**:
- Report is self-contained — a guild leader can act on it without querying the system further
- JSON format contains all the same data as text format, structured for programmatic consumption

---

## RC-02: Player Detail Report

**Purpose**: Produce a detailed readiness report for a single player.

**Input**:
- Single `ReadinessClassification`
- The player's `CharacterProfile`
- Relevant `EconomicRecommendation` data

**Output**: Per-player detail view.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | `guild-ops report player --contract <id> --character <character_id>` |
| Sections | 1. Player header (name, class, role, status). 2. Gear summary (equipped items with iLevels). 3. Consumable checklist (have/need). 4. Enchantment checklist (have/need). 5. Violations with remediation. 6. Estimated cost to reach READY |
| Consumable checklist | For each required consumable: checkmark if in inventory with quantity met, X if missing. Include market price and recommendation |
| Enchantment checklist | For each required enchantment slot: checkmark if enchanted with an accepted enchant, X if missing or wrong enchant |
| Cost to READY | Sum of market costs for all missing mandatory items. "N/A" if any item has no market data |

---

## RC-03: Economic Detail Report

**Purpose**: Produce a detailed economic analysis for all deficit items.

**Input**:
- All `EconomicRecommendation` records for a contract
- `PriceTrend` data for each item

**Output**: Market-focused report.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | `guild-ops report economics --contract <id>` |
| Sections | 1. Summary (total deficit count, total estimated cost). 2. Per-item detail ordered by estimated cost descending |
| Per-item detail | Item name, deficit quantity, current price, average price, trend direction, percent vs average, recommendation, confidence, estimated cost |
| Trend visualization | Text-based indicator: ↑ RISING, → STABLE, ↓ FALLING |
| Threshold display | Show the configured buy/wait thresholds so the reader understands why each recommendation was made |

---

## RC-04: Output Format Contract

**Purpose**: Define the supported output formats and their guarantees.

**Contract**:

| Format | Description | Guarantee |
| ------ | ----------- | --------- |
| text | Human-readable plain text with aligned columns and sections | Deterministic output for same inputs |
| json | Machine-readable JSON with all data fields | Valid JSON, deterministic key ordering (sorted keys), deterministic array ordering as defined in each engine contract |

All reports MUST support both formats via `--format text|json` flag. Default is `text`.

# Quickstart: Guild Operations System

**Branch**: `001-define-specification-driven` | **Date**: 2026-04-08

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.11 or later
- pip (Python package manager)
- A WoW TBC Classic realm name and faction for AH queries
- (Optional) Blizzard API client credentials for live AH data

## Setup

```bash
# Clone and enter project
cd sample_project

# Create virtual environment
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # Linux/macOS
# or: .venv\Scripts\activate  # Windows

# Install dependencies
pip install -e ".[dev]"

# Initialize database with TBC item catalog
guild-ops init --realm "Whitemane" --faction "Horde"
```

## Core Workflow

### 1. Define a Raid Contract

Create a YAML file describing what your raid requires:

```yaml
# contracts/karazhan_tuesday.yaml
raid_name: "Karazhan"
scheduled_time: "2026-04-15T20:00:00"
go_threshold_percent: 80
drift_detection_enabled: true

role_requirements:
  TANK:
    required_consumables:
      - item_id: 22849  # Ironshield Potion
        quantity: 5
      - item_id: 28103  # Adept's Elixir
        quantity: 1
    required_enchantments:
      - slot: CHEST
        accepted_enchant_ids: [2661]  # Exceptional Stats
    min_average_item_level: 115

  HEALER:
    required_consumables:
      - item_id: 22861  # Flask of Mighty Restoration
        quantity: 1
      - item_id: 27657  # Blackened Basilisk
        quantity: 20
    required_enchantments:
      - slot: WEAPON
        accepted_enchant_ids: [2669]  # Major Healing

  RANGED_DPS:
    required_consumables:
      - item_id: 22866  # Flask of Supreme Power
        quantity: 1
    optional_consumables:
      - item_id: 22844  # Dark Rune
        quantity: 10

  MELEE_DPS:
    required_consumables:
      - item_id: 22854  # Flask of Relentless Assault
        quantity: 1

guild_materials:
  - item_id: 17020  # Arcane Powder
    total_quantity: 40
    purpose: "Arcane Brilliance reagent"

roster:
  - character_id: "toadsworth-whitemane"
    assigned_role: TANK
    confirmed: true
  - character_id: "ribbittia-whitemane"
    assigned_role: HEALER
    confirmed: true
```

Load it:

```bash
guild-ops contract load contracts/karazhan_tuesday.yaml
guild-ops contract activate karazhan-2026-04-15
```

### 2. Ingest Data

```bash
# Ingest auction house snapshot (from Blizzard API)
guild-ops ingest auctions --source blizzard

# Or from a file
guild-ops ingest auctions --source file --path data/ah_snapshot.json

# Ingest character profiles (from addon export)
guild-ops ingest profiles exports/raid_profiles.json

# Ingest guild bank
guild-ops ingest guild-bank exports/guild_bank.json
```

### 3. Evaluate

```bash
# Run full evaluation (deficits + economics + readiness)
guild-ops evaluate --contract karazhan-2026-04-15

# Or run components individually
guild-ops evaluate deficits --contract karazhan-2026-04-15
guild-ops evaluate economics --contract karazhan-2026-04-15
guild-ops evaluate readiness --contract karazhan-2026-04-15
```

### 4. Review Reports

```bash
# Guild readiness summary
guild-ops report summary --contract karazhan-2026-04-15

# Single player detail
guild-ops report player --contract karazhan-2026-04-15 --character toadsworth-whitemane

# Economic analysis
guild-ops report economics --contract karazhan-2026-04-15

# JSON output for scripts
guild-ops report summary --contract karazhan-2026-04-15 --format json
```

### 5. Drift Detection (Optional)

```bash
# Capture a baseline (auto-captured on contract activation)
guild-ops baseline capture --contract karazhan-2026-04-15

# Check for drift after new data ingestion
guild-ops evaluate drift --contract karazhan-2026-04-15
```

## Running Tests

```bash
# All tests
pytest

# Unit tests only
pytest tests/unit/

# Integration tests
pytest tests/integration/

# Determinism test (runs evaluation twice, asserts identical output)
pytest tests/integration/test_determinism.py
```

## Configuration

The system reads configuration from `config.yaml` in the project root:

```yaml
realm: "Whitemane"
faction: "Horde"

economic_thresholds:
  buy_threshold: 0.85      # Price ≤ 85% of average → BUY
  wait_threshold: 1.30     # Price ≥ 130% of average → WAIT
  lookback_days: 7         # Moving average window

data_freshness:
  max_snapshot_age_hours: 24
  max_profile_age_hours: 48

blizzard_api:
  # Credentials loaded from environment variables:
  # BLIZZARD_CLIENT_ID, BLIZZARD_CLIENT_SECRET
  region: "us"
  namespace: "dynamic-classic-us"
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
| -------- | ------- |
| BLIZZARD_CLIENT_ID | Blizzard API client ID |
| BLIZZARD_CLIENT_SECRET | Blizzard API client secret |
| GUILD_OPS_DB_PATH | Override default database location |
| GUILD_OPS_CONFIG | Override default config file path |

These contracts define the interfaces through which external data enters the system. All ingestion adapters MUST conform to these contracts regardless of data source.

---

**Purpose**: Ingest a point-in-time snapshot of auction house listings for a specific realm.

**Input**: Raw data from one of the supported sources (Blizzard API JSON, NexusHub API JSON, addon export file).

**Output**: A validated `AuctionHouseSnapshot` entity persisted to storage.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops ingest auctions` or scheduled trigger |
| Input format | JSON (API response) or structured text (addon export) |
| Required fields per listing | item_id (integer), quantity (integer ≥ 1), buyout_copper (integer ≥ 0) |
| Derived fields | unit_price_copper = buyout_copper / quantity (integer division, rounded down) |
| Deduplication | Listings with duplicate auction_id within a single snapshot are merged (last wins) |
| Staleness check | If snapshot timestamp is older than configured `max_snapshot_age_hours` (default: 24), emit WARNING and tag snapshot as STALE |
| Zero-listing items | If a required item (per any active contract) has zero listings, record it with a synthetic "UNAVAILABLE" marker |
| Idempotency | Re-ingesting the same snapshot (by snapshot_id or timestamp) replaces the previous version |
| Error handling | Malformed listings are skipped with a logged warning; ingestion continues. If >50% of listings are malformed, the entire snapshot is rejected |

**Postconditions**:
- Snapshot is queryable by realm and timestamp
- Price trend computation can be triggered against the new snapshot
- Items referenced in listings exist in the item catalog (unknown item_ids logged as warnings but not rejected)

---

## IC-02: Character Profile Ingestion

**Purpose**: Ingest a character's current state (gear, inventory, enchantments, talents, professions).

**Input**: Addon export file (JSON or parsed Lua SavedVariables) or Blizzard API response.

**Output**: A validated `CharacterProfile` entity persisted to storage.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops ingest profiles <file>` |
| Input format | JSON file containing one or more character profiles |
| Required fields | character_name, realm, class_name, level, equipped_items |
| Optional fields | inventory_consumables, enchantments, talent_build, professions, officer_notes |
| Identity | character_id derived as lowercase `{name}-{realm}` |
| Deduplication | If a profile for the same character_id exists, it is replaced entirely (last-write-wins) |
| Role assignment | assigned_role may be provided in the import or set/overridden via roster management |
| Staleness | last_updated field is mandatory; system flags profiles older than configured `max_profile_age_hours` (default: 48) |
| Partial data | Profiles with missing optional fields are accepted; missing inventory_consumables means consumable readiness cannot be fully evaluated (flagged in violations) |

**Postconditions**:
- Profile is queryable by character_id
- Profile is linkable to roster entries
- If inventory_consumables is missing, readiness evaluation will produce a WARNING-level violation noting incomplete data

---

## IC-03: Guild Bank Ingestion

**Purpose**: Ingest the current contents of the guild bank.

**Input**: Addon export or manual entry (JSON/YAML file).

**Output**: Guild bank inventory stored as a set of `InventoryItem` records tagged with source "GUILD_BANK".

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops ingest guild-bank <file>` |
| Input format | JSON or YAML file listing items and quantities |
| Required fields per entry | item_id (integer), quantity (integer ≥ 1) |
| Replacement | Each ingestion replaces the entire guild bank inventory (not incremental) |
| Validation | item_ids are checked against the item catalog; unknown items are logged as warnings |

**Postconditions**:
- Guild bank contents are available to deficit computation
- Deficit engine subtracts guild bank supply from total requirements

---

## IC-04: Raid Contract Ingestion

**Purpose**: Create or update a raid preparation contract from a YAML definition file.

**Input**: YAML file conforming to the contract schema.

**Output**: A validated `RaidPreparationContract` entity persisted to storage.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | CLI command `guild-ops contract load <file>` |
| Input format | YAML file |
| Required sections | raid_name, scheduled_time, role_requirements (at least TANK and HEALER), roster |
| Item validation | All item_ids and enchant_ids referenced in requirements MUST exist in the item catalog; unknown references cause rejection with specific error listing each unknown |
| Role validation | All roles referenced in roster entries MUST have a corresponding role_requirements section |
| Duplicate check | A contract with the same contract_id replaces the previous version |
| Status default | New contracts are created with status DRAFT |

**Postconditions**:
- Contract is queryable by contract_id
- Activating the contract (status → ACTIVE) enables readiness evaluation and deficit computation against it
- All referenced items and enchantments are resolvable in the catalog

toolUseResult.content:
# Evaluation Contracts

**System**: Guild Operations System | **Date**: 2026-04-08

These contracts define the interfaces for the core computation engines: economic recommendation, readiness classification, and drift detection.

---

## EC-01: Economic Recommendation Engine

**Purpose**: For each item deficit identified against an active raid contract, produce a deterministic economic recommendation (BUY, FARM, WAIT).

**Input**: 
- Active `RaidPreparationContract` with computed deficits
- Latest `AuctionHouseSnapshot` and `PriceTrend` data
- Configured economic thresholds

**Output**: List of `EconomicRecommendation` records, one per deficit item.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered by `guild-ops evaluate economics --contract <id>` |
| Determinism | Same inputs (contract, snapshot, thresholds) MUST produce identical outputs. No randomness, no system-clock dependency in logic (timestamp only in metadata) |
| Threshold defaults | buy_threshold: 0.85 (price ≤ 85% of average → BUY), wait_threshold: 1.30 (price ≥ 130% of average → WAIT) |
| Decision logic | 1. If no market data: action = FARM, confidence = LOW. 2. If percent_vs_average ≤ buy_threshold: action = BUY. 3. If percent_vs_average ≥ wait_threshold AND trend = RISING: action = WAIT. 4. If is_farmable: action = FARM. 5. Else: action = WAIT |
| Confidence | HIGH: ≥ 7 snapshots in lookback window. MEDIUM: 3-6 snapshots. LOW: < 3 snapshots or no data |
| Cost estimation | estimated_cost_copper = deficit_quantity × current_price_copper. If no market data, estimated_cost = 0 with note "no market data" |
| Ordering | Results ordered by estimated_cost_copper descending (most expensive deficits first) |
| Missing data | Items with no price data get action = FARM, confidence = LOW, reason = "No market data available" |

**Postconditions**:
- Every deficit item has exactly one recommendation
- Recommendations are usable by readiness remediation guidance (IC-02 → EC-01 linkage)

---

## EC-02: Readiness Classification Engine

**Purpose**: Evaluate each rostered player's character profile against the raid contract and classify as READY, WARNING, or NOT_READY.

**Input**:
- Active `RaidPreparationContract`
- `CharacterProfile` for each rostered player
- Current `EconomicRecommendation` data (for remediation guidance)

**Output**: List of `ReadinessClassification` records, one per rostered player.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered by `guild-ops evaluate readiness --contract <id>` |
| Determinism | Same inputs MUST produce identical outputs |
| Evaluation scope | Each player is evaluated against the role_requirements for their assigned_role in the contract |
| Classification rules | NOT_READY: ≥ 1 mandatory violation. WARNING: 0 mandatory violations, ≥ 1 advisory violation. READY: 0 violations |
| Consumable check | For each required_consumable: verify player's inventory_consumables contains item_id with quantity ≥ required. Missing → MANDATORY violation |
| Optional consumable check | For each optional_consumable: verify inventory. Missing → ADVISORY violation |
| Enchantment check | For each required_enchantment: verify player's enchantments for the specified slot contains one of accepted_enchant_ids. Missing → MANDATORY violation |
| Gear check | If min_average_item_level set: compute average iLevel across equipped slots. Below minimum → MANDATORY violation. Per-slot minimums checked individually |
| No profile data | If a rostered player has no CharacterProfile: status = NOT_READY, single violation: category=PROFILE, severity=MANDATORY, description="No profile data available" |
| Stale profile | If last_updated exceeds max_profile_age_hours: add ADVISORY violation noting data staleness |
| Missing inventory | If inventory_consumables is null/missing on profile: add ADVISORY violation "Consumable inventory data not available — cannot verify consumable readiness" |
| Remediation | Each violation includes a remediation string. For consumable/enchantment violations, remediation includes market info from EconomicRecommendation if available |
| Ordering | Results ordered by: NOT_READY first, then WARNING, then READY. Within each group, alphabetical by character_name |

**Postconditions**:
- Every rostered player has exactly one classification
- Zero false READY classifications (a player with any mandatory violation is never READY)
- Classifications are consumable by the summary report and drift detection engines

---

## EC-03: Deficit Computation Engine

**Purpose**: Compute guild-level material deficits by comparing total requirements against available supply.

**Input**:
- Active `RaidPreparationContract` (requirements + roster)
- All `CharacterProfile` records for rostered players (inventory)
- Guild bank inventory

**Output**: List of deficit records (item_id, total_required, total_available, deficit_quantity).

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered internally by economic evaluation, or directly via `guild-ops evaluate deficits --contract <id>` |
| Total required | Sum of required quantities across all rostered players for their assigned role, plus guild_materials |
| Total available | Sum of: (a) each player's inventory_consumables matching the item, (b) guild bank quantities |
| Deficit | max(0, total_required - total_available) |
| No double-counting | A player's inventory items count toward their own requirements AND the guild total, but are not counted twice |
| Zero deficits | Items where deficit = 0 are included in output (shows the guild has enough) |
| Determinism | Same inputs produce identical deficit counts |

**Postconditions**:
- Deficit list feeds into EC-01 (economic recommendations)
- Every item referenced in contract requirements appears in the deficit output

---

## EC-04: Drift Detection Engine

**Purpose**: Compare current evaluation state against a stored baseline and report changes.

**Input**:
- A stored `Baseline` (previous evaluation snapshot)
- Current `ReadinessClassification` and `EconomicRecommendation` outputs

**Output**: List of `DriftEvent` records.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | Triggered by `guild-ops evaluate drift --contract <id>` (only if drift_detection_enabled on contract) |
| Baseline creation | A baseline is automatically captured when a contract transitions to ACTIVE, and can be manually refreshed via `guild-ops baseline capture --contract <id>` |
| Player drift | For each player: if classification status changed (e.g., READY → NOT_READY), emit a PLAYER_READINESS drift event listing old status, new status, and the new/changed violations |
| Economic drift | For each deficit item: if recommendation action changed (e.g., BUY → WAIT), emit an ECONOMIC drift event listing old action, new action, price change, and impact on estimated cost |
| No drift | If no changes detected, return empty list (not an error) |
| Disabled | If drift_detection_enabled is false on the contract, the engine returns immediately with no output |
| Determinism | Same baseline + same current state = same drift events |

**Postconditions**:
- Drift events are reportable in the summary
- Drift events do not modify the baseline (baseline is immutable until explicitly refreshed)

toolUseResult.content:
# Reporting Contracts

**System**: Guild Operations System | **Date**: 2026-04-08

These contracts define the output interfaces — how computed results are presented to users.

---

## RC-01: Guild Readiness Summary Report

**Purpose**: Produce a consolidated report showing overall guild readiness for a specific raid contract.

**Input**:
- `ReadinessClassification` results for all rostered players
- `EconomicRecommendation` results for all deficit items
- `DriftEvent` list (if drift detection enabled)
- Contract's go_threshold_percent

**Output**: Structured report (text or JSON format).

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | `guild-ops report summary --contract <id>` with optional `--format text|json` |
| Sections | 1. Header (raid name, time, roster size). 2. Go/No-Go verdict. 3. Player status breakdown. 4. Deficit & economic summary. 5. Drift alerts (if any). 6. Action items |
| Go/No-Go logic | GO if (count of READY / total rostered) × 100 ≥ go_threshold_percent. Otherwise NO-GO |
| Player breakdown | Count and percentage for each status (READY, WARNING, NOT_READY). List each non-READY player with their violations |
| Economic summary | Total estimated gold cost to close all deficits. Each deficit item with quantity, unit price, total cost, and recommendation |
| Drift section | Only included if drift_detection_enabled and drift events exist |
| Action items | Prioritized list of remediation actions, ordered by impact (NOT_READY players first, then highest-cost deficits) |
| Determinism | Same inputs produce byte-identical text output (stable ordering, no timestamps in body except evaluation time in header) |
| Gold formatting | All copper values rendered as gold/silver/copper (e.g., 385012 copper → "38g 50s 12c") |

**Postconditions**:
- Report is self-contained — a guild leader can act on it without querying the system further
- JSON format contains all the same data as text format, structured for programmatic consumption

---

## RC-02: Player Detail Report

**Purpose**: Produce a detailed readiness report for a single player.

**Input**:
- Single `ReadinessClassification`
- The player's `CharacterProfile`
- Relevant `EconomicRecommendation` data

**Output**: Per-player detail view.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | `guild-ops report player --contract <id> --character <character_id>` |
| Sections | 1. Player header (name, class, role, status). 2. Gear summary (equipped items with iLevels). 3. Consumable checklist (have/need). 4. Enchantment checklist (have/need). 5. Violations with remediation. 6. Estimated cost to reach READY |
| Consumable checklist | For each required consumable: checkmark if in inventory with quantity met, X if missing. Include market price and recommendation |
| Enchantment checklist | For each required enchantment slot: checkmark if enchanted with an accepted enchant, X if missing or wrong enchant |
| Cost to READY | Sum of market costs for all missing mandatory items. "N/A" if any item has no market data |

---

## RC-03: Economic Detail Report

**Purpose**: Produce a detailed economic analysis for all deficit items.

**Input**:
- All `EconomicRecommendation` records for a contract
- `PriceTrend` data for each item

**Output**: Market-focused report.

**Contract**:

| Aspect | Requirement |
| ------ | ----------- |
| Caller | `guild-ops report economics --contract <id>` |
| Sections | 1. Summary (total deficit count, total estimated cost). 2. Per-item detail ordered by estimated cost descending |
| Per-item detail | Item name, deficit quantity, current price, average price, trend direction, percent vs average, recommendation, confidence, estimated cost |
| Trend visualization | Text-based indicator: ↑ RISING, → STABLE, ↓ FALLING |
| Threshold display | Show the configured buy/wait thresholds so the reader understands why each recommendation was made |

---

## RC-04: Output Format Contract

**Purpose**: Define the supported output formats and their guarantees.

**Contract**:

| Format | Description | Guarantee |
| ------ | ----------- | --------- |
| text | Human-readable plain text with aligned columns and sections | Deterministic output for same inputs |
| json | Machine-readable JSON with all data fields | Valid JSON, deterministic key ordering (sorted keys), deterministic array ordering as defined in each engine contract |

All reports MUST support both formats via `--format text|json` flag. Default is `text`.
